artifact_or_test_id: the live reload error; server_impl top-of-module eviction set; test_mcp_reload_evicts_context_efficiency_dependency's pinned set
command_or_fixture: live wf_reload_mcp call, 2026-07-21; eviction-block source read
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Any future module-level sibling dependency added to server_impl silently breaks hot reload the same way.
expected: A hot reload picks up both the new server_impl AND its refreshed module-level dependencies.
known_bad_detection_method: reproduced live; the updated pinned-set test fails against the pre-repair set
limitations: The runner-layer eviction in server.py stays wave_lint_lib-only; the in-module block is the authoritative mechanism and the one importlib.reload re-executes.
observed: Live wf_reload_mcp failure with the exact unpack error immediately after the vocabulary repair; mechanism confirmed by reading the eviction block (server_impl.py ~line 27) and the reload path in server.py (evicts only wave_lint_lib at the runner layer).
proposition: server_impl's top-of-module reload-eviction set (wave_lint_lib*, review_evidence, context_efficiency) does not include the new public_contract dependency, so wf_reload_mcp re-executed the new server_impl (five-name tuple unpacking) against the STALE cached two-value SEARCH_MODES and crashed: 'not enough values to unpack (expected 5, got 2)'.
safety_and_authorization: Local reload and source reads only.

disposition_rationale: One-line repair (public_contract joins the in-module eviction set, which re-executes at the TOP of every reload, before the import that crashed — making the live session self-healing on retry) plus the pinned-set test update; the deeper lesson (new module-level sibling imports must join the eviction set) recorded for memory.

The 7452 'index_missing' is a diagnostic CODE, not a fallback_reason — out of scope.
proposition: LEXICAL_FALLBACK_REASONS still omits two emitted public values — model_unavailable and index_missing, passed through _fts_fallback and _degraded_citation_pass as fallback_reason in docs, code, and code_ask search — and _fts_degraded_serve emits bare store_absent/query_failed literals through its failure_reason field; the source pin catches only direct fallback_reason = '...' assignments, so all three producer shapes evade it.

disposition_rationale: Bounded repair: the tuple grows to the true five values; every producer shape (call arguments, comparisons, the failure_reason dict values) consumes the aliases; the docstring contract drops its quoted value strings so the pin can enforce ZERO quoted occurrences of any canonical reason value in server_impl — indirection-proof by construction.
